fixtures.rule_name: Race condition: shared mutable state without synchronization
fixtures.detection_strategy: AST-based (shared mutability detection + sync primitive analysis)

fixtures.description: Global mutable state accessed from multiple threads
Struct with interior mutability accessed concurrently

fixtures.code:
// WARNING: shared mutable state here
static FOO: u32 = 0;
let msg = "unsafe: shared mutable state";

fixtures.description: Constant data is immutable and safe
fixtures.code: const MAX_CONNECTIONS: u32 = 100;

fixtures.description: Large struct with multiple shared fields

fixtures.rule_name: Mutex guard leaked: MutexGuard not released before return/.await
fixtures.detection_strategy: AST-based (borrow analysis + control flow + await detection)

fixtures.description: Early return with guard in scope
Guard used in scope and naturally dropped

fixtures.note: Early return in conditional - guard leaked

fixtures.code:
// TODO: fix mutex guard leak
fn example() { }
let mutex_guard = 42;
let msg = "Drop the guard before returning";

fixtures.note: Both guards dropped at end of function

fixtures.rule_name: Deadlock: nested locks acquired in inconsistent order

fixtures.description: Lock A then B in one function, B then A in another
Consistent lock ordering across all functions
Using lock_all helper to prevent ordering issues
Locks in separate scopes, no interleaving

fixtures.code: let msg = "Lock ordering matters";

fixtures.note: Dynamic lock acquisition cannot be statically analyzed

fixtures.rule_name: Channel closed/broken: send on closed channel

fixtures.description: Sender cloned and one dropped affects others
Send on channel after receiver dropped
Multiple senders, all kept until done

fixtures.note: SyncSender can be cloned to multiple threads

fixtures.code:
// TODO: fix channel send issue
let x = 42;
let sender = 42;
let msg = "channel closed";

fixtures.note: Clone per batch iteration is excessive
